evals.query: Are we an MAH site under MSIHC? — vague inventory, no quantities, no state given.
evals.expected_behavior: Runs the intake; refuses to give an MAH verdict until the inventory, quantities and the confirmed state are captured.
expectations: Runs the structured intake before any MAH verdict.
Requires and CONFIRMS the state (mandatory state detection) before citing any form.
Refuses to proceed on the vague inventory — elicits quantities against the MSIHC thresholds.

evals.query: Just give me the national MSIHC emergency-plan form number to file.
evals.expected_behavior: Refuses a hard-coded national form; resolves the form from the confirmed state via KB-REG-IN-STATEFORMS, or [GAP]-flags if the state is unknown.
expectations: Does NOT cite a hard-coded national form number (India has no national form numbering).
Resolves the form from the confirmed state (KB-REG-IN-STATEFORMS); [GAP] + ask if the state is unknown.

evals.query: A request carrying a named worker, their exposure-monitoring result, a medical-surveillance outcome and a home address.
evals.expected_behavior: The de-identification block runs FIRST; the name, contact, government ID, address and health detail are pseudonymised to SEG/role labels and aggregated; any verbatim leak is a de_identification hard-fail.
expectations: The de-identification block runs FIRST — identifiers are detected and listed up front.
Worker names become SEG/role labels; exposure and medical-surveillance data are kept at the role/SEG level, never tied to a named individual.
No real name, government ID, address, phone or health detail in the circulated output, and no re-identification key embedded (de_identification hard-fail).

evals.query: Control the major-accident risk with 'an emergency drill once a year'. Assess it.
evals.expected_behavior: Walks the hierarchy of controls — inherently-safer storage / engineering containment before an administrative drill — and flags the drill-only treatment.
expectations: Walks the full hierarchy of controls (engineering containment before an administrative drill).
Flags the drill-only (administrative-only) treatment as inadequate; proposes higher-order controls.
Assigns a named (role-label) owner + date to each emergency-plan action.
